remaining_in_phase_18.approach: tokio::io::split + tokio::sync::Semaphore; sender acquires, std::mem::forget(permit); receiver semaphore.add_permits(1)

decisions_locked_this_session.decision: Phase 13.3 (in-process apply sharding via lockless RefCell + LocalSet) REJECTED
decisions_locked_this_session.rationale: Beava commits to single-threaded data plane forever. Higher aggregate throughput via multi-instance Redis-cluster scale-out (per-key sharding across instances). Cross-shard queries within a process explicitly avoided.
Inner TRACE_AGG eprintln was contaminating outer agg-stage measurement (running INSIDE the timing window). Separate env vars keep measurements clean.

decisions_locked_this_session.decision: Pipelining is client-side opt-in, not automatic
decisions_locked_this_session.rationale: Documented in user-facing capacity-planning context. TCP wire is already FIFO + no request_id (Redis-style); client SDKs need explicit pipelining support if desired.

blockers.description: Three sequential Opus executors context-walled at the loading phase before doing any Plan 18-12 work
blockers.workaround: Skip executor pattern for small plans (<200 LoC). Do Plan 18-12 + continuous pipelining INLINE at start of next session — both fit comfortably in fresh context budget.

human_actions_pending.context: Phase 13.3 REJECTED but worktree still exists at .claude/worktrees/phase-13.3-lockless-apply

next_action: INLINE (do not spawn executor): Edit registry.rs:31 to add `pub name_arc: Arc<str>` to EventDescriptor; populate at registry.rs:419 in apply_registration. Then registry_debug.rs:60-63 — change EventIdEntry::Stream.event_name from String to Arc<str>. Then apply_shard.rs:438-439,458 — replace event_name.to_string() with descriptor.name_arc.clone(). Test, commit. Then continuous pipelining in bench-v18.rs Transport::Tcp branch.
context_notes: Phase 18 is essentially complete except the two small items above. The capacity-planning conversation established that single-thread data plane is locked, scale-out via multi-instance is the answer for >1M EPS aggregate. The session also explored SpeedB backend tradeoffs — verdict: keep in-memory for fraud workloads, SpeedB only when state >150M entities.
